name: pipelines
context: Define task dependency graphs in turbo.json â€” dependsOn, outputs, inputs, caching rules, and execution order
info: This skill covers defining and managing tasks in turbo.json's tasks object. Tasks are the core of Turborepo â€” they map to package.json scripts and define how work flows through your monorepo. Key concepts include topological dependencies (^build ensures libraries build before apps), same-package dependencies (test depends on build within each workspace), workspace-specific dependencies (web#lint depends on utils#build), output declarations for caching, input patterns for cache invalidation, persistent tasks for dev servers, and the transit node pattern for type checking. Agents use this skill when adding new tasks, configuring build pipelines, optimizing task parallelism, or debugging dependency ordering. See [[turbo/config]] for global configuration and [[turbo/caching]] for cache behavior.
triggers: make test depend on build in turbo
set up dev server in turbo

sections.title: dependsOn syntax
sections.body: The `dependsOn` array defines task execution order. `^task` is a topological dependency (run in package dependencies first). `task` is a same-package dependency (run within the same workspace first). `package#task` is a workspace-specific dependency (run a specific package's task first). Empty array `[]` means no dependencies â€” runs immediately. Tasks without `dependsOn` also have no dependencies.

sections.title: Output caching
sections.body: The `outputs` array declares file patterns to cache after successful completion. Globs are relative to workspace root. Use `!` to exclude patterns (e.g., `!.next/cache/**`). If outputs is empty or omitted, no files are cached but the task result (exit code, logs) is still cached. Common patterns: `dist/**` for compiled output, `.next/**` for Next.js, `storybook-static/**` for Storybook, `coverage/**` for test coverage.

sections.title: Input patterns and cache invalidation
sections.body: The `inputs` array defines which file changes invalidate the cache. By default, all files tracked by git (minus outputs) are inputs. Override with explicit patterns: `["src/**", "tsconfig.json"]`. Use `$TURBO_DEFAULT$` to start from defaults and add exclusions: `["$TURBO_DEFAULT$", "!README.md"]`. Narrower inputs mean more cache hits â€” a docs change won't invalidate a build if README.md is excluded from inputs.

sections.title: Persistent and interactive tasks
sections.body: Set `persistent: true` for long-running tasks like dev servers, watchers, or REPL processes. Persistent tasks never cache and cannot be depended on by other tasks (Turborepo would deadlock). Use `with` to run sibling tasks alongside a persistent task. Set `interactive: true` for tasks needing stdin (implies persistent). Set `interruptible: true` to allow turbo watch to restart the task on file changes.

sections.title: Root workspace tasks
sections.body: Prefix task names with `//` to run scripts from the root package.json: `//#lint:root`, `//#format`. This is useful for repository-wide linting, formatting, or other root-level scripts. Root tasks follow the same configuration options as workspace tasks.

sections.title: Transit node pattern
sections.body: The transit pattern optimizes type checking across workspaces. Define a `transit` task with `dependsOn: ["^transit"]` and have `check-types` depend on `transit`. This gives the type checker package awareness (it knows about dependency outputs) without forcing sequential execution of the type-check itself across the dependency graph. The transit task has no script â€” it's a graph-only node.
